channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Near-perfect genome sequencing in medical genetics
item.description: This Perspective introduces near-perfect genome sequencing, which encompasses diploid genome assembly, pangenome references and artificial intelligence-driven variant interpretation, and proposes a roadmap toward its clinical implementation.

item.title: Midjourney's ambitious full-body 'scan spa' sounds almost too good to be true
item.description: A company known for its AI image generation has made a surprise pivot – to consumer healthcare. Midjourney has announced it will offer a "new form of medical imaging" using echolocation to map the body, in a day-spa setting, with the first center to open to t…

item.title: Who will control africa’s AI infrastructure, and at what cost?
item.description: Data centres and AI systems are expanding across Africa, bringing new pressure on energy and resources.

item.title: Generative ai-enabled clinical decision support system in primary care: a pragmatic, cluster-randomized trial
item.description: A pragmatic cluster-randomized trial found that ChatGPT-4o-assisted decision support in Kenyan primary care facilities did not significantly reduce 14-day treatment failure over usual care.

item.title: Chinese cybersecurity company claims it’s built a better-than-mythos bug finder
item.description: Qihoo 360, which the US has banned, says it’s needed as a deterrent to weaponized Anthropic models

item.title: Anthropic thinks its own success is key to making AI safe
item.description: Anthropic's critics argue it's rapidly accumulating power. The company says that's what responsible AI development looks like.

item.title: Development of APH003─a Highly Potent, Selective, and Orally Bioavailable IRAK4 PROTAC Degrader for the Treatment of Inflammatory Diseases
item.description: APH003 is a highly potent and selective PROTAC degrader of IRAK4 developed for treating inflammatory diseases, with demonstrated oral bioavailability. The compound was developed through collaborative research involving Sun Yat-Sen University and ApicHope Pharmaceutical Group. PROTAC technology enabl

item.title: FDA Proposes Rule That Would Help Hold Foreign Tobacco Product Manufacturers Accountable, Protect Public Health
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a proposed rule that, if finalized, would help protect the public health of Americans, including youth, by strengthening the agency’s ability to efficiently identify illegal foreign tobacco products — including youth-appealing e-cigarettes — and con

item.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
item.description: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

item.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building

item.title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
item.description: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.

item.title: OpenAI’s Jalapeño chip is Big Tech’s spiciest move away from Nvidia
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building

item.title: Multifunctional nanotherapeutics for tumor microenvironment modulation in solid tumor therapy
item.description: Multifunctional nanotherapeutics represent an emerging approach for modulating the tumor microenvironment in solid tumor treatment. These nanotechnology-based therapeutics are designed to target and alter the cellular and molecular conditions surrounding tumors, potentially improving therapeutic eff

item.title: Solo-maxxing: how tech taught gen z to monetise being alone
item.description: We can no longer pretend that the bridge between generations is invisible, because it stands before us, growing wider every day, harder to cross and harder even to name. Nor can we deny that technology has had its hand in this rupture, since it has not merely…

item.title: How atlantic health cut legal document search time by 42% with amazon bedrock metadata filtering
item.description: In this post, you learn how to implement metadata-filtered RAG for multi-entity document search using Amazon Bedrock Agents and Amazon OpenSearch Service.

item.title: Metabolic determinants of cancer immunotherapy outcomes identified by plasma profiling
item.description: Mass-spectrometry-based metabolomic analysis of plasma samples from multiple cohorts of patients treated with immunotherapy across five distinct tumor types, followed by machine learning enabled identification of metabolic signatures, as well as functional ex…

item.title: Advances and challenges of splicing prediction with AI
item.description: Predicting splicing outcomes is a central challenge in precision medicine. This Review traces the evolution of computational approaches from early statistical heuristics to modern artificial intelligence frameworks to characterize splicing events.

item.title: The cpu's growing role in agentic AI infrastructure
item.description: PARTNER CONTENT: As agentic AI systems scale across cloud and datacenter environments, CPUs remain the control plane coordinating performance and efficiency.

item.title: Divergent effects of pathological α-synuclein truncations and mutations on phase separation
item.description: This study investigates how different pathological forms of α-synuclein—specifically truncations and mutations—produce divergent effects on phase separation, a process implicated in protein aggregation relevant to neurodegenerative disease. The research characterizes the biophysical behavior of thes

item.title: STAT+: FDA’s breakthrough pipeline fills up with generative AI devices
item.description: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: FDA drops enforcement against Whoop, more AI devices get FDA 'breakthrough' label, and more.

item.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
item.description: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

item.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
item.description: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
item.description: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

item.title: Data-driven surrogates of rational design enable antimicrobial peptide optimization
item.description: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 25 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01258-0 Rising pathogen drug resistance makes next-generation antimicrobial peptides a global priority. Generative AI accelerates discovery by rapidly proposing new peptides with high therapeutic potential. The key q

item.title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
item.description: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.

item.title: Chan zuckerberg biohub to open new rare disease funding round
item.description: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative's Biohub will offer new grants to the rare disease community while expanding its AI drug repurposing partnership with Every Cure.

item.title: Europe’s AI wake-up call: cybersecurity threats, sovereignty fears, and a growing demand for roi dominated vivatech
item.description: As 180,000 attendees descended on Paris, the conversations revolved around cybersecurity risks, European dependence on U.S. AI, and whether the returns on AI investment are actually materializing.

item.title: Two more gemini researchers are leaving google for anthropic
item.description: Two leading artificial intelligence researchers at Google are planning to leave for Anthropic, according to a Bloomberg report on June 24, the latest departures from the team behind Gemini and the second pair to head for the Claude maker inside a week. Jonas …

item.title: Google gains spot on dow jones while losing street cred in AI
item.description: Google shares have been hammered recently as two top artificial intelligence researchers spurned the company to join OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: AI in production conference summary (2026)
item.description: “AI In Production 2026”, a new conference hosted by Jumping Rivers, was held on 4-5 June, 2026. The conference touched on many of the intricacies of using AI in software products, as part of the developer toolkit, and at the wider us... Continue reading: …

item.title: Social media activities to enhance the visibility of long-term invasive ventilation: a content and visibility analysis from the privent study
item.description: Background Despite the increasing number of patients receiving long-term invasive mechanical ventilation (IMV) and the social importance of this issue, it is largely neglected in the public perception. One aim of the PRIVENT project was to raise public awaren…

item.title: Early potential safety signals for gliptins and gliflozins using real-world pharmacy data compared to spontaneous reporting
item.description: Background Dipeptidyl peptidase 4 inhibitors (DPP4i, gliptins) and sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 inhibitors (SGLT2i, gliflozins) are two oral antidiabetic drug classes that have been widely used in recent decades. Their pharmacological actions have led to t…

item.title: Nigeria needs n500bn investment to scale digital health — fg
item.description: The Minister of State for Health and Social Welfare, Dr Iziaq Adekunle Salako, disclosed this on Thursday at the opening ceremony of the 6th Africa Digital Health Summit (ADHS 2026) in Abuja, where he called on pension fund administrators, development partner…

item.title: 5 integration checks to make before software rollouts harden
item.description: Software rollouts can lock in before teams know how data, handoffs, automation and context will work. These checks help expose the gaps earlier.

item.title: Artificial intelligence in emergency department triage: a scoping review
item.description: Background Triage in emergency departments (ED) is a critical process for prioritizing care and ensuring clinical safety. However, current triage systems often exhibit vulnerabilities that compromise the efficiency and quality of healthcare delivery. Artifici…

item.title: Assessing the sustainability of rwanda’s mass drug administration program for schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminthiasis: a mixed-methods application of the program sustainability assessment tool
item.description: Author summary This study evaluated the sustainability of Rwanda’s mass drug administration (MDA) program for schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminthiasis. Using interviews and a standardized sustainability tool (PSAT), we assessed the program’s current…

item.title: Bioinformatics analysis of pyroptosis-related differentially expressed genes in sepsis and diabetes mellitus
item.description: Sepsis and diabetes mellitus (DM) are major global health issues with high morbidity and mortality, necessitating new molecular insights for improved treatments. In this study, pyroptosis-related differentially expressed genes (PRDEGs) linked to both diseases…

item.title: Speed guidance strategy at intersections based on platoon recognition in connected and autonomous vehicles environments
item.description: To alleviate the issues of widespread traffic congestion and low passage efficiency at urban signalised intersections, which result in increased vehicle energy consumption, this paper proposes a speed guidance strategy based on platoon recognition in connecte…

item.title: Bhramar mukherjee: what can the data tell Us?
item.description: Bhramar Mukherjee: What Can the Data Tell Us?Anonymous (not verified) Thu, 06/25/2026 - 08:39 Howie and Harlan are joined by Yale School of Public Health biostatistician Bhramar Mukherjee to discuss how statisticians help researchers turn observations into …

item.title: Openai will delay gpt-5.6 after trump administration request
item.description: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that i…

item.title: Openai says employees moving beyond chat to agents
item.description: Codex, it's not just for developers, really

item.title: Openai says 97.9 percent of its employees are now using agents
item.description: Codex, it's not just for developers, really

item.title: Anthropic's claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by chatgpt | techcrunch
item.description: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

item.title: General intuition's $2.3b bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world | techcrunch
item.description: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

item.title: Parker conrad knows which employees are worth their AI spend and says rippling can help you, too | techcrunch
item.description: "There were employees doing things like, 'Claude is so helpful for me — it analyzes my calendar and my email and puts together a plan for me,'" he says. "That person was spending at a run rate of $30,000 a year for this."

item.title: The download: europe’s heat wave hits the grid, and ibm’s chip targets moore’s law
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants Europe is in the middle of a record-breaking heat wave, and the grid …

item.title: Notion mail shuts down amid agent takeover | techcrunch
item.description: The company said it is discontinuing its email inbox in favor of its AI agent offering as users are increasingly handing over the reins of their email to the agents.

item.title: Recovery has to keep up with AI
item.description: SPONSORED POST: Why an AI-era recovery architecture looks different, with Eon's Gonen Stein

item.title: Apple raises mac and ipad prices, spares iphone for now
item.description: Apple raises prices of MacBook Air and Pro along with iPad Air and Pro

item.title: World cup teams are in a race for AI dominance
item.description: This year, FIFA is providing an AI agent that any team can use. Is it enough to level the playing field or will future winners be determined by which team can afford the best tools?
